---
title: "MA641 Research Project"
author: "Yash Tanaji Bhosale"
date: "2025-11-13"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=TRUE}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)

#load data
gold_raw <- read.csv("XAU_USD Historical Data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

#cleaning and converting cols 
gold <- gold_raw %>%
  mutate(
    Date  = mdy(Date),                 # convert to Date type
    Price = as.numeric(gsub(",", "", Price))  # remove commas ā†’ convert to numeric
  ) %>%
  arrange(Date) %>%
  select(Date, Price)

#summary of price col
summary(gold$Price)

#Missing value check
sum(is.na(gold$Price))
sum(is.na(gold$Date))

#Plot the raw price series
ggplot(gold, aes(x = Date, y = Price)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkgoldenrod2") +
  labs(
    title = "Daily Gold Spot Price (XAU/USD), 2015ā€“Present",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Gold Price (USD)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
library(moments)

#Basic descriptive statistics
mean_price  <- mean(gold$Price, na.rm = TRUE)
var_price   <- var(gold$Price, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_price    <- sd(gold$Price, na.rm = TRUE)
skew_price  <- skewness(gold$Price, na.rm = TRUE)
kurt_price  <- kurtosis(gold$Price, na.rm = TRUE)

cat("Mean:", mean_price, "\n")
cat("Variance:", var_price, "\n")
cat("Standard Deviation:", sd_price, "\n")
cat("Skewness:", skew_price, "\n")
cat("Kurtosis:", kurt_price, "\n")

```
```{r}
ggplot(gold, aes(x = Price)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..),
                 bins = 50,
                 fill = "gold",
                 alpha = 0.6,
                 color = "black") +
  geom_density(color = "red", size = 1.2) +
  labs(title = "Histogram + Density of Gold Prices",
       x = "Gold Price (USD)",
       y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
library(tseries)
library(zoo)

gold$roll_mean <- rollmean(gold$Price, k = 30, fill = NA, align = "right")
gold$roll_var  <- rollapply(gold$Price, width = 30, FUN = var, fill = NA, align = "right")

# 2.2 Plot rolling mean & variance
ggplot(gold, aes(x = Date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Price), color = "goldenrod2", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_line(aes(y = roll_mean), color = "blue", size = 1) +
  labs(title = "Rolling Mean (30-Day) ā€“ Gold Price",
       y = "Price") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(gold, aes(x = Date, y = roll_var)) +
  geom_line(color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Rolling Variance (30-Day) ā€“ Gold Price",
       y = "Variance") +
  theme_minimal()

#ADF test on RAW PRICE
adf_test_raw <- adf.test(gold$Price, alternative = "stationary")
adf_test_raw
```

```{r}
library(tseries)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# 3.1 Apply log transform
gold$log_price <- log(gold$Price)

# 3.2 First differencing ( log(price))
gold <- gold %>%
  mutate(
    diff_log_price = log_price - lag(log_price)
  )

# 3.3 Create a clean dataset without the first NA row
gold_diff <- gold %>% filter(!is.na(diff_log_price))

# 3.4 Plot differenced series
ggplot(gold_diff, aes(x = Date, y = diff_log_price)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  labs(
    title = "Differenced Log Gold Price (Approx Returns)",
    x = "Date",
    y = " log(price)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# 3.5 ADF test on differenced log prices
adf_test_diff <- adf.test(gold_diff$diff_log_price, alternative = "stationary")
adf_test_diff
```

```{r}
library(forecast)

# 4.1 ACF plot
acf(gold_diff$diff_log_price,
    main = "ACF of Differenced Log Gold Price")

# 4.2 PACF plot
pacf(gold_diff$diff_log_price,
     main = "PACF of Differenced Log Gold Price")
```
```{r}
library(forecast)

# Use the stationary series
y <- gold_diff$diff_log_price

# 5.1 ARIMA(0,1,1) -- MA(1)
fit_011 <- Arima(gold$Price, order = c(0,1,1))
summary(fit_011)

# 5.2 ARIMA(1,1,0) -- AR(1)
fit_110 <- Arima(gold$Price, order = c(1,1,0))
summary(fit_110)

# 5.3 ARIMA(1,1,1) -- ARMA mixed
fit_111 <- Arima(gold$Price, order = c(1,1,1))
summary(fit_111)

# 5.4 ARIMA(0,1,0) -- Random Walk baseline
fit_010 <- Arima(gold$Price, order = c(0,1,0))
summary(fit_010)
```
```{r}
library(forecast)

# Extract residuals
res_010 <- residuals(fit_010)
res_011 <- residuals(fit_011)
res_110 <- residuals(fit_110)

# 6.1 Plot residuals (random noise?)
autoplot(res_010) +
  ggtitle("Residuals of ARIMA(0,1,0)")

# 6.2 ACF of residuals (should have no significant spikes)
Acf(res_010, main = "ACF of Residuals ā€” ARIMA(0,1,0)")

# 6.3 Ljungā€“Box test
Box.test(res_010, lag = 20, type = "Ljung-Box")
```
```{r}
#6.4 Histogram of residuals
hist(res_010, breaks = 40, col = "gold", main = "Histogram of Residuals")

# 6.5 Q-Q plot (should be roughly straight line)
qqnorm(res_010)
qqline(res_010, col = "red")
```
```{r}
library(forecast)

# Use the final model fit_010 from Step 5
# Forecast next 30 days
forecast_30 <- forecast(fit_010, h = 30)

# Print forecast values
forecast_30

# Plot forecast
autoplot(forecast_30) +
  labs(
    title = "30-Day Forecast ā€” ARIMA(0,1,0) for Gold Price",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Gold Price (USD)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
library(forecast)

# Use the final model fit_010 from Step 5
# Forecast next 60 days
forecast_60 <- forecast(fit_010, h = 60)

# Print forecast values
forecast_60

# Plot forecast
autoplot(forecast_60) +
  labs(
    title = "60-Day Forecast ā€” ARIMA(0,1,0) for Gold Price",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Gold Price (USD)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
